title: Rich Citrusy Venezuelan Chicken Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic chicken dish featuring dark soy sauce and a blend of citrus flavors, perfect for a special occasion or date night, and is halal-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dark soy sauce, orange juice, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the chicken and onion, then add the dried dates.
Cover the pot and bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the chicken and sauce over rice or with some crusty bread, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
